questions.text: Which is the smallest state in India by area?
questions.explanation: Goa is the smallest state of India by area.

questions.text: The Statue of Unity is a statue of which leader?
questions.explanation: The Statue of Unity is of Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel.

questions.text: The President lives in the ____ Bhavan in New Delhi.
questions.explanation: The President of India lives in Rashtrapati Bhavan.

questions.text: Mount Everest is the highest mountain peak in the world.
questions.explanation: Mount Everest is the world's highest peak. True.

questions.text: Which is the hottest planet in our solar system?
questions.explanation: Venus is the hottest planet due to its thick atmosphere.

questions.text: What is the chemical symbol for water?
questions.explanation: Water is H2O (two hydrogen, one oxygen).

questions.text: The force that pulls objects towards the Earth is called ____.
questions.explanation: Gravity pulls objects toward the Earth.

questions.text: Sound cannot travel without a medium like air or water.
questions.explanation: Sound needs a medium to travel. True.

questions.text: Which is the largest ocean in the world?
questions.explanation: The Pacific Ocean is the largest ocean.

questions.text: Which is the largest continent?
questions.explanation: Asia is the largest continent.

questions.text: There are ____ continents in the world.
questions.explanation: There are 7 continents.

questions.text: The Sahara is the largest hot desert in the world.
questions.explanation: The Sahara Desert is the largest hot desert. True.

questions.text: Which is the only mammal that can truly fly?
questions.explanation: The bat is the only mammal that can truly fly.

questions.text: The ____ is the largest bird in the world.
questions.explanation: The ostrich is the largest bird.

questions.text: A dolphin is a fish.
questions.explanation: A dolphin is a mammal, not a fish. False.

questions.text: Who invented the electric bulb?
questions.explanation: Thomas Edison invented the practical electric bulb.

questions.text: ____ Bell invented the telephone.
questions.explanation: Alexander Graham Bell invented the telephone.

questions.text: C.V. Raman was an Indian scientist who won the Nobel Prize.
questions.explanation: C.V. Raman won the Nobel Prize in Physics. True.

questions.text: Which Indian city is called the 'Pink City'?
questions.explanation: Jaipur is known as the Pink City.

questions.text: The ____ is the national river of India.
questions.explanation: The Ganga is the national river of India.

questions.text: Gandhi Jayanti is celebrated on 2nd October.
questions.explanation: Gandhi Jayanti is on 2 October. True.

questions.text: How many colours are there in a rainbow?
questions.explanation: A rainbow has 7 colours (VIBGYOR).

questions.text: The first colour of the rainbow (from top) is ____.
questions.explanation: Red is the top (outer) colour of a rainbow.

questions.text: The Sun is a planet.
questions.explanation: The Sun is a star, not a planet. False.

questions.text: How many players are there in a football team on the field?
questions.explanation: A football team has 11 players on the field.

questions.text: The ____ is given to the winner in the Olympics for first place.
questions.explanation: The gold medal is for first place.

questions.text: Chess is a game played on a board with 64 squares.
questions.explanation: A chessboard has 64 squares. True.

questions.text: Which country is known as the Land of the Rising Sun?
questions.explanation: Japan is called the Land of the Rising Sun.

questions.text: The Eiffel Tower is located in the city of ____.
questions.explanation: The Eiffel Tower is in Paris, France.

questions.text: We should switch off lights and fans when not in use.
questions.explanation: It saves electricity. True.

questions.text: Which is the ambulance emergency number in India?
questions.explanation: 102 and 108 are ambulance emergency numbers in India.
